things.name: Riga Old Town (Vecrīga)
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage medieval old town with Gothic churches, narrow lanes and Riga Cathedral

things.name: Art Nouveau District
things.description: The world's highest concentration of Art Nouveau buildings — over 800 facades in Riga's centre

things.name: Riga Central Market
things.description: Europe's largest market housed in five converted WWI Zeppelin hangars — a Latvian institution

things.name: Latvian National Museum of Art
things.description: Grand 1905 neoclassical building housing the finest collection of Latvian art

things.name: Freedom Monument
things.description: Beloved symbol of Latvian independence with the Milda statue — changing of the guard daily

things.name: Riga Black Balsam Experience
things.description: Latvia's national herbal bitters — taste it in bars across the Old Town and visit the Latvijas Balzams distillery

things.name: Museum of the Occupation of Latvia
things.description: Powerful museum documenting Soviet and Nazi occupations — one of the Baltic states' most visited sites

things.name: Ethnographic Open Air Museum
things.description: 100-hectare open air museum with 118 original Latvian rural buildings from the 17th to 20th centuries

things.name: Jurmala Beach Resort
things.description: Baltic Sea resort town 25 km west with wooden Art Nouveau villas and white-sand beaches

things.name: St Peter's Church Tower
things.description: Iconic 123-metre Gothic steeple with a viewing platform offering the finest views of Riga's rooftops
